title: Compliance Trending ‚Äî Regulatory Change Feed Impact Score on In-Scope Controls

compliance.assurance_rationale: Phase-D retagged from meta-multi placeholder to a concrete ISO 27001 clause. ¬ß9.1 Monitoring/measurement ‚Äî regulatory-change impact scoring is a form of monitoring the external context. Assurance level set to 'contributing' pending SME review per Phase E of the plan.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO 27001 9.1 (Monitoring, measurement, analysis, evaluation) is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.9.9: Compliance Trending ‚Äî Regulatory Change Feed Impact Score on In-Scope Controls.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.9.9' running on sourcetype reg:change_item and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Phase-D retagged from meta-multi placeholder to a concrete SOC 2 clause. SOC 2 CC3.1 Risk Assessment ‚Äî regulatory change is a primary risk-assessment input. Assurance level set to 'contributing' pending SME review per Phase E of the plan.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SOC 2 CC3.1 (Risk assessment) is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.9.9: Compliance Trending ‚Äî Regulatory Change Feed Impact Score on In-Scope Controls.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.9.9' running on sourcetype reg:change_item and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

dataSources: `index=grc` `sourcetype="reg:change_item"` (item_id, jurisdiction, effective_date, summary_tokens, impacted_control_ids, impact_score, _time) (Splunkbase 1621 ‚Äî Splunk CIM Add-on)
app: HTTP Event Collector from regulatory intelligence vendor or manual curator tool
spl:
index=grc sourcetype="reg:change_item" earliest=-730d
| eval eff=strptime(effective_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where eff>now() OR eff>relative_time(now(),"-90d@d")
| timechart span=30d sum(impact_score) as rolling_impact by jurisdiction
| trendline sma3(rolling_impact) as impact_trend
description: Laws and supervisory guidance change continuously. Scoring incoming regulatory change items by how many in-scope controls they touch‚Äîand trending that score‚Äîhelps compliance prioritize policy updates, training, and control amendments before effective dates.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001, SOC 2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc and sourcetypes reg:change_item. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Maintain mapping table from `impacted_control_ids` to owners; (2) human validates `impact_score`‚Äîdo not fully automate legal severity; (3) integrate effective-date countdown alerts; (4) deduplicate vendor spam with `item_id`; (5) cross-link to policy management tickets. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=reg:change_item`.
visualization: Line chart (rolling_impact + impact_trend), Table (upcoming items), Single value (next-90d sum). Key fields: `eff`.
knownFalsePositives:
1. **Assessment cycle alignment** ‚Äî Control assessments following annual cycles may show brief non-current periods between assessment completion and report publication.

2. **Finding remediation within SLA** ‚Äî Open findings within their documented remediation timeline are being addressed per policy, not neglected or overlooked.

3. **Framework version transitions** ‚Äî Migration between control framework versions (ISO 27001:2013 to 2022) creates temporary mapping gaps during the documented transition period.

4. **Accepted residual risk items** ‚Äî Findings formally accepted through the risk management governance process with documented compensating controls and management sign-off.

5. **Evidence collection cadence differences** ‚Äî Controls with different evidence generation frequencies (real-time vs. quarterly) show varying currency in compliance dashboards.

**Operational suppression:** Maintain a lookup table (`iso27001_approved_exceptions.csv`) mapping known legitimate activities by control owner, risk acceptance reference, and expiry date. Filter the SPL results against this lookup to suppress documented exceptions and reduce alert fatigue. Review and rotate entries at each ISMS management review cycle.
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

‚Ä¢ **Splunk**: Enterprise ‚â•9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
‚Ä¢ **Indexes**: `grc` must exist on all indexers with retention matching the regulation's minimum (PCI: 1y, HIPAA: 6y, NIST 800-53: 1-3y per FIPS 199, SOX: 7y). Set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` accordingly in `indexes.conf`.
‚Ä¢ **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `9.1`. The control objective is: "Evidence that ISO 27001 9.1 (Monitoring, measurement, analysis, evaluation) is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.9.9: Compliance Trending ‚Äî Regulatory Change Feed Impact Score on In-Scope Controls." ‚Äî every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection

Data collection is bespoke for this UC ‚Äî no single canonical TA parses every source the detection relies on. Route data into `grc` under `reg:change_item`, and document the ingestion path in the UC runbook so future engineers know where each field originates.


5 operational considerations apply to the rollout of this detection:


‚Ä¢ **Hint 1:** Maintain mapping table from `impacted_control_ids` to owners. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability ‚Äî document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

‚Ä¢ **Hint 2:** human validates `impact_score`‚Äîdo not fully automate legal severity. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability ‚Äî document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

‚Ä¢ **Hint 3:** integrate effective-date countdown alerts. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability ‚Äî document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

‚Ä¢ **Hint 4:** deduplicate vendor spam with `item_id`. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability ‚Äî document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

‚Ä¢ **Hint 5:** cross-link to policy management tickets. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability ‚Äî document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

## Step 2 ‚Äî Implement the search

This search aggregates `sum()` across the last 730 days, grouped by `jurisdiction`. For "Compliance Trending ‚Äî Regulatory Change Feed Impact Score on In-Scope Controls," the aggregation exposes temporal structure that a single point-in-time reading cannot ‚Äî the compliance question is about behaviour over a period, not at an instant. The aggregation approach is chosen to produce one result row per analytical unit, which is the typical requirement for compliance dashboarding and report generation.

Why aggregate at search time rather than rely on raw events: compliance reporting requires summarisation to be defensible and stable. A raw-event dashboard can show different counts depending on the exact second it was queried; an aggregated result rounded to the UC's schedule produces consistent numbers across the reporting period. If the downstream consumer (GRC platform, regulator submission) expects stable daily/weekly figures, schedule this search to write to a summary index and read from the summary for reports ‚Äî that way the numbers quoted on Monday match what was quoted on Wednesday.

Cardinality vigilance: if `jurisdiction` has very high cardinality (e.g. grouping by a free-text `user_agent` or `url` field), the result set explodes and the search becomes slow. Check cardinality with `| stats dc(jurisdiction) as cardinality` before scheduling; if >100000 distinct groups, either narrow the search, pre-filter to the top N values, or redesign the search to aggregate at a coarser grain.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.9.9: Compliance Trending ‚Äî Regulatory Change Feed Impact Score on]
cron_schedule = 0 */6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -730d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.9.9,reg=ISO_27001
```

## Step 3 ‚Äî Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=grc sourcetype=reg:change_item earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data ‚Äî it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `eff`. Run:

```spl
index=grc sourcetype=reg:change_item earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table eff
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.9.9: ISO 27001 evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 ‚Äî Line Chart (`rolling_impact + impact_trend`):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 2 ‚Äî Drill-through table (`upcoming items`):** Full-width bottom row. Enable row-level drilldown to a secondary dashboard that expands each violation with its raw events, timeline, and related context. This is the panel the auditor will actually read ‚Äî optimise it for scannability (avoid huge wrap-around fields).

**Panel 3 ‚Äî Single-value KPI (`next-90d sum`):** Place at top-left. Configure `underLabel` with the regulation clause (e.g. 'ISO 27001 current violation count') and set a red colour range for any non-zero value. A zero reading is the steady state for a healthy control.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y ‚Äî the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 ‚Äî Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**Search returns no results when violations are expected:** Verify time-window alignment, index existence, and field extractions in that order. Most 'missing results' are simpler configuration issues before they're detection-logic issues.

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**ISO 27001-specific failure modes:**

‚Ä¢ Statement of Applicability (SoA) drift: ISO 27001 requires a SoA listing which Annex A controls are applicable and which are excluded with justification. A detection for a control excluded in the SoA adds noise to the audit evidence pile; reconcile UC-to-SoA mapping annually. Maintain `iso27001_soa.csv` with current applicability.

‚Ä¢ Context of the organisation (Cl. 4): controls calibrated for a 50-person company's threat model do not suit a 5000-person multinational's. Detection thresholds, log retention, and escalation paths must align with the organisation's ISMS scope ‚Äî a single 'enterprise default' for ISO 27001 is a tell-tale sign of a cookie-cutter implementation.

‚Ä¢ Internal audit programme (Cl. 9.2) evidence: detections that support internal audits must produce records that can be attached to audit evidence packs. Tag the alert payload with audit_cycle=YYYY-HN so evidence bundling is trivial.

‚Ä¢ Management review inputs (Cl. 9.3): control effectiveness metrics must be reviewable at management review. A detection that only raises alerts (no trend metric) cannot be brought to the management meeting; schedule a companion report that produces trend data per control.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for Compliance Trending ‚Äî Regulatory Change Feed Impact Score on In-Scope Controls. We watch trends so leaders see improvement, not a one-time snapshot.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Create a test control with last assessment date exceeding the defined review period (e.g., 13 months for annual controls); verify the compliance search identifies the overdue assessment and alerts the control owner.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Record a test control assessment completed within the defined review period with satisfactory results and evidence attached; verify no overdue alert fires and the control shows as current in the dashboard.

evidence: Control effectiveness report showing assessment dates, testing results, evidence links, finding status, and management review decisions archived for certification audit evidence.
exclusions: Controls within their defined testing cadence; newly implemented controls in maturation period; framework version transition gaps during documented migration; formally accepted residual risks with governance approval.
